language_distribution.Python: secondary (8 repos, primary for builder + issue-manager)

enkai.description: AI Feature Builder - Turn feature specs into small, reviewable PRs
enkai.role: Main monorepo: CLI, dashboard, infra/cdk, schemas, lambda functions, Slack integration
findings: Still contains infra/cdk directory that overlaps with enkai-infra
Still contains dashboard directory that overlaps with enkai-monitor
Has schemas/ directory with schema definitions also found in satellites
5 GitHub Actions workflows — most mature CI/CD in the org

enkai-infra.description: Shared AWS infrastructure for the Enkai platform (VPC, ECS, DynamoDB, SQS, S3, Route53, Cognito)
enkai-infra.role: Centralized CDK infrastructure: 30 stacks covering all platform services

key_issues.title: Deploy shared infrastructure stacks to AWS
Set up self-hosted GitHub Actions runner on MacBook
[EPIC] Migrate from CodeBuild/CodePipeline to Self-Hosted GitHub Actions

findings: 30 CDK stacks — comprehensive but high blast radius per deploy
CodeBuild CI/CD while enkai uses GitHub Actions (split CI/CD)
Issue #31 acknowledges need to migrate to GitHub Actions
Issue #36 indicates stacks not yet fully deployed

enkai-builder.description: Autonomous AI feature builder - transforms issue specs into production-ready PRs
enkai-builder.role: Core build agent: receives SQS jobs, executes code generation, creates PRs
findings: Largest Python codebase (6.3MB) — core business logic
Contains shared_types/ that duplicates code in enkai-issue-manager
Contains schemas/ with JSON Schema contracts (9+ schemas)
Has Bedrock AgentCore integration (agentcore/context/validation/schemas.py)
No CI/CD pipeline configured
Single contributor — bus factor risk

enkai-issue-manager.description: AI-powered GitHub issue analysis, enrichment, decomposition, and job dispatch service
enkai-issue-manager.role: Issue intake: analyzes GitHub issues, decomposes into work items, dispatches to SQS
findings: Contains shared_types/ that duplicates code in enkai-builder
Contains schemas/v1/ with different schema set than enkai-builder
Uses CodeBuild while enkai uses GitHub Actions
Single contributor — bus factor risk

enkai-monitor.role: Dashboard and monitoring: platform health, build status, metrics
findings: Overlaps with enkai monorepo's dashboard/ directory
No CI/CD pipeline configured
Not pushed since January — potentially stale

enkai-nextjs-template.description: Next.js template repository optimized for Enkai AI-powered development
enkai-nextjs-template.role: Project scaffolding: starter template for new Enkai-powered projects
findings: Minimal template — only 5KB
Not updated since creation

frank.description: Run Claude Code in containers with web UI, worktree isolation, and AWS ECS deployment
frank.role: Container runtime: ECS Fargate containers for AI coding agents with web UI

key_issues.title: Community skills download fails due to pedro post-checkout hook
Migrate from CodePipeline to GitHub Actions self-hosted runner

findings: Multi-language (Go + TS + Python + Shell) — high maintenance surface
Issue #101 confirms need to migrate CI/CD to GitHub Actions
Issue #100 confirms planned migration to enkai-infra shared stacks
Actively maintained (last push yesterday)

pedro.description: Community skills framework for AI coding agents
pedro.role: Skills ecosystem: reusable skill definitions, hooks, and scripts for AI agents
findings: Pure Python — clean dependency surface
Actively maintained
Skills are consumed by frank containers at startup

metis.description: AI-powered development toolkit built on the Pedro framework
metis.role: Dev toolkit: code analysis, quality checking, development automation
findings: Largest supporting repo by disk (64MB — likely includes data/models)
Dual-language (TS + Python) — pattern matches enkai platform
Newest supporting repo (created Feb 16)

template-factory.description: Template Factory - built on Pedro scaffolding
template-factory.role: Project scaffolding: generates new project structures from templates
findings: Built on Pedro framework — consistent with ecosystem pattern
Actively maintained

janus.description: Centralized deployment orchestration service - accepts deployment requests, orchestrates pipelines, learns failure patterns, opens GitHub issues for code-level fixes
janus.role: Planned: deployment orchestration with self-healing capabilities
findings: Empty repo — planned but not started
Ambitious description suggests self-healing deployment orchestration
Created same day as enkai-infra — part of the multirepo extraction

brandassador.role: Brand management: content generation and brand consistency tooling
findings: Highest open issue count in the org (18)
Most recently pushed repo (today)
Built on Pedro framework

schema_drift_risk.description: JSON Schema definitions exist in 3 repos without a shared registry

details.enkai_builder: schemas/ directory with 9+ schemas (job, result, evidence, handoff, workitem, SDLC contracts, StepFunctions)
details.enkai_issue_manager: schemas/v1/ directory with 4 schemas (checkpoint, issue_state, job_dispatch, webhook_event)

schema_drift_risk.risk: Schema changes in one repo can break consumers in other repos with no compile-time or CI check

shared_types_duplication.description: shared_types/ directory exists in both enkai-builder and enkai-issue-manager with overlapping Python types
shared_types_duplication.risk: Changes to shared types require manual synchronization across repos; divergence causes runtime failures

monorepo_to_multirepo_incomplete.description: enkai monorepo still contains infra/cdk and dashboard directories despite enkai-infra and enkai-monitor existing as separate repos
monorepo_to_multirepo_incomplete.risk: Unclear which copy is authoritative; developers may edit the wrong location

split_ci_cd.description: CI/CD is split across GitHub Actions and CodeBuild with most satellite repos having no CI at all
split_ci_cd.migration_planned: enkai-infra issue #31 tracks migration to self-hosted GitHub Actions
split_ci_cd.risk: No automated testing or deployment for 7 active repos; manual deployments increase error rate

bus_factor.description: Several critical repos have only 1 contributor
bus_factor.risk: Core platform components (builder, issue-manager) depend on a single person's knowledge

dependency_graph.description: Logical dependency flow of the Enkai platform
dependency_graph.flow: GitHub webhook → enkai-issue-manager → SQS → enkai-builder → PR pipeline
dependency_graph.monitoring: enkai-monitor provides dashboard and health checks
dependency_graph.container_runtime: frank provides ECS container management for AI agents
dependency_graph.skills: pedro provides reusable agent skills consumed by frank containers

key_strengths: Formal JSON Schema contracts for inter-service communication (9+ schemas in enkai-builder)
Comprehensive CDK infrastructure (30 stacks in enkai-infra covering all AWS services)
Bedrock AgentCore integration in enkai-builder for AI agent orchestration
Self-hosted GitHub Actions runner planned (enkai-infra issue #35)
Pedro skills ecosystem enables reusable agent capabilities across projects
Frank container runtime provides production-grade ECS Fargate isolation
Template-factory and Pedro framework enable consistent project scaffolding
SQS-based async architecture (issue-manager → builder) provides natural backpressure
key_risks: Schema drift across 3 repos with no registry or version management
shared_types/ copy-pasted between enkai-builder and enkai-issue-manager
Monorepo-to-multirepo migration incomplete (enkai retains infra/ and dashboard/)
Split CI/CD: GitHub Actions in enkai, CodeBuild in satellites, no CI in 7 repos
Janus repo empty — deployment orchestration planned but not started
Bus factor of 1 for critical repos (enkai-builder, enkai-issue-manager)
Test coverage unknown — no CI means no enforced coverage floor
